verdict: ESCALATE — blocked on upstream tt-mlir #3370 + 28-head TP constraint

iterations.fail: sdy.collective_permute not lowerable (ShardyToStableHLO) + 'op requires same type for all operands and results'
iterations.root_cause: 2D batch+model reshard emits CollectivePermuteOp; not implemented in tt-mlir

iterations.fail: reshape.568: number of output elements (14680064) doesn't match expected (1835008) [ratio 8x]
iterations.root_cause: 8-way model-axis sharding invalid: num_attention_heads=28 not divisible by 8

iterations.fail: TT_FATAL Out of Memory: 771162112 B DRAM buffer, free 72122304 B (chip ~full)
iterations.root_cause: Compiled + ran. Per-chip weights = 34.9GB/4 = 8.73 GiB of 12 GiB; ~2-3 GiB headroom insufficient for runtime buffers. OOM on a ttnn.add (binary_ng).
iterations.note: Captured inputs are tiny (hidden_states [1,64,2,2]); OOM is weight-pressure, NOT reducible input-activation size.

why_blocked: Only pure model-axis TP degree that (a) divides num_attention_heads=28 and (b) fits <= 8 chips is 4. 4-way leaves insufficient DRAM for runtime buffers. 8-way would fit weights (4.36 GiB/chip) but is invalid (28 % 8 != 0). 2D FSDP-style reshard (e.g. (2,4)) would shard weights further but emits collective_permute -> blocked by tt-mlir #3370.

options.reason: 28/7=4 heads/chip valid; 34.9/7=4.99 GiB/chip fits. Untested: non-power-of-2 mesh, 1 idle chip, collective topology mapping risk
unblocks 2D weight sharding -> halves per-chip weight

recommendation: Mark transformer ESCALATED (upstream-blocked). Best near-term experiment if pursued: 7-way mesh (1,7) bf16 — head-valid and weight-fits, but mesh/topology support unverified. Otherwise revisit when tt-mlir #3370 lands.
